profile.markdown:
**Full Name:** Martis Shura

**Alias:** Asura King

**Origin:** Shura Clan, Arcapada Universe

**Species:** Asura

**Gender:** Male

**Weapons:** Asura's Teeth

**Abilities:** Enhanced Combat, Enhanced Speed

**Traits:** Domineeringly Prideful, Violent Completely

story.markdown:
The Shura clan have thrived in isolation for countless generations, in the Arcapada universe. Their men, renowned for being the world's bravest fighters, and their women famed for being the world's greatest beauties. At the heart of the Shura clan's beliefs lies the legend of the Three Thousand Worlds. The legends speaks of three thousand secret worlds, which upon entering, one must face various levels of psychic and physical trials and advance to world after world upon victory. The people of the Shura clan had discovered a way to enter these worlds, and only those who have conquered over three hundred worlds could be granted the title of Asura, raising them to the level of a demigod in the eyes of their people. However, in the innumerable eons of Shura history, none managed to conquer the fabled three thousand world, until Martis appeared.

With ambition and determination capable of shaking the very heavens, Martis trained every day of his life to become stronger, tirelessly advancing through the secret worlds, each personal breakthrough against the legions of demons granting him a sense of unique personal satisfaction. No one knew how many years passed before Martis reached the final and most hard core secret world. However, his desire for glory drove him onward. After numerous defeats, he fell into despair, but even then, he refused to give up. He transformed his sheer thirst for victory into his twin blades of righteous fury, now known as the Asura's Teeth, and for countless days and nights, battled against the demons of the final world with strength and determination that had never been seen before on the mortal plane. He finally became the first Asura to defeat the three thousand secret worlds and has ever since been regarded as the 'Asura King', ruling, more than tens of milions of aspiring Asura.

chapters.title: Shura Clan and the Three Thousand Worlds

content.markdown: The Shura clan have thrived in isolation for countless generations, their men renowned for being the world's bravest fighters, and their women famed for being the world's greatest beauties. At the heart of the Shura clan's beliefs lies the legend of the Three Thousand Worlds. The legend speaks of three thousand secret worlds, which upon entering, one must face various levels of psychic trials and advance to world after world upon victory. The people of the Shura clan have discovered a way to enter these worlds, and only those who have conquered over three hundred worlds are granted the title of "Asura", raising them to the level of a demigod in the eyes of their people.

However, in the innumerable eons of Shura history, none managed to conquer the fabled three thousand worlds, until Martis appeared. With ambition and determination capable of shaking the very heavens, Martis trained every day of his life to become stronger, tirelessly advancing through the secret worlds, each personal breakthrough against the legions of demons granting him a sense of unique personal satisfaction. No one knows how many years passed before Martis reached the final secret world, but his desire for glory drove him onwards. After numerous defeats, he fell into despair, but even then, he refused to give up. He transformed his sheer thirst for victory into twin blades of righteous fury, now known as the Asura's Teeth, and for countless days and nights, battled against the demons of the final world with strength and determination never seen before on the mortal plane. He finally became the first Asura to defeat the three thousand secret worlds and has since been regarded as 'King Asura', ruling tens of millions of aspiring Asura.

details.markdown: Each time Martis uses a skill, his Attack Speed will be increased by 30%, up to a maximum of 120%. This effect lasts 4 seconds. He gains (6 × Hero Level) extra Attack at full stacks.

Martis draws enemies to a fan-shaped area in front of him, dealing physical damage and slowing them by 40% for 2 seconds.

- Base Damage: 350 / 410 / 470 / 530 / 590 / 650
- Damage type: Physical Damage scaling with extra Physical Attack
- Additional effect: Movement Speed reduction on enemies

This skill makes Martis move forward a little, thus it counts as a blink skill despite not having a blink tag.

This skill is disabled while inside Minsitthar's ultimate.

This skill enables Phoveus' ultimate.

Martis dashes in the target direction three times, each time dealing damage to enemies hit. Enemies hit by the first and third strike are also knocked back. The attack direction can be changed once using the Joystick.

Use Again: Martis dashes in the target direction, dealing damage to enemies in his path and knocking them back. Martis gains Control Immunity and 60% Damage Reduction when casting this skill.

- First phase Base Damage: 225 / 240 / 255 / 270 / 285 / 300
- Second phase Extra Damage: 300 / 320 / 340 / 360 / 380 / 400
- Effects: Blink, Control Immunity, Damage Reduction, knockback crowd control

Cooldown starts after casting the 1st or 2nd phase of the skill.

The 1st phase will trigger 3 stacks of Asura's Wrath, but only 1 stack on the Unique Passive of Brute Force Breastplate.

Interruption: This skill can only be interrupted during charging.

This skill is not immune to Suppression crowd control effects.

This skill can be disabled by Rooted effects and can be affected by knockback.

Upon casting, there will be a slight delay before the first slash occurs that does a quick knock-up effect to all opponents in front and beside Martis. This is then immediately followed up by another slash that only does damage to all opponents in front and beside Martis. After the second slash, there will be another slight delay before the last slash occurs that does a quick knock-up effect to all opponents near Martis.

The second phase of the skill immediately knocks all opponents away from Martis upon making contact on his body.

Martis lunges at a designated enemy hero, dealing damage (deals True Damage if the enemy's HP is below 50%). If the enemy is killed by this skill, Martis can cast the skill again within 10 seconds and gains 100% extra Movement Speed that gradually decays over 5 seconds. Each successive cast also increases the skill's damage by 15% (up to 30%).

- Base Damage: 800 / 900 / 1000
- Damage type: Physical Damage, becomes True Damage against enemies below 50% HP
- Effects: Movement Speed bonus, skill cooldown reset potential, executes low-HP enemies

When an enemy Hero's HP drops below 40%, a mark will appear beneath them.

There are two animations for his ultimate: he stabs the enemy with his blade after dashing in the first attempt; he jumps and stabs the enemy from above with his pair of blades in subsequent slays. Both of the animation does not differ in-game.

If Martis successfully eliminates a Hero equipped with Immortality, and the Unique Passive Immortal is still applied, he is able to recast the skill once more when the target Hero is resurrected by the Unique Passive Immortal.

The bonuses are also applied by enemy Heroes resurrected from Faramis' Cult Altar.

The cooldown only starts upon eliminating, or attempting to eliminate, an enemy Hero with this skill.

This skill can be interrupted during dashing.

This skill can be disabled by Rooted effects.

trivia.markdown: The name Martis is derived from the Roman god of war, Mars. In Roman mythology, Mars was the god of war, battle, and violence, often associated with aggression and conflict. The name Martis retains this connection to warrior-like strength, power, and fighting prowess.
Martis' Quote "3000 worlds and not a single worthy foe" was a bluff, he was actually defeated several times and was even put in despair.

The term "3000 Worlds" is a hyperbolic reference to the 3000 Trials, where individuals must face and clear each trial (World or Level). Those who successfully complete 300 of these levels are considered to be at the level of a Demi-God in the eyes of the Asura Clan.

After completing the 3000 Trials, Martis encountered the Demi-God Gatotkaca, and a fierce battle ensued. Both were equally matched in strength, and their fight alone created an entrance to the Timerift. After the 3000 Trials, Martis met a Demi-God Gatotkaca and fought, both were equal in strength and their fight alone opened an entrance to the timerift.
Martis is canonically one of the most powerful hero in MLBB. Gatotkaca being his only equal and to stop the Ashura wrath.

markdown:
# Profile

**Full Name:** Martis Shura

**Alias:** Asura King

**Origin:** Shura Clan, Arcapada Universe

**Species:** Asura

**Gender:** Male

**Weapons:** Asura's Teeth

**Abilities:** Enhanced Combat, Enhanced Speed

**Traits:** Domineeringly Prideful, Violent Completely

# Story

The Shura clan have thrived in isolation for countless generations, in the Arcapada universe. Their men, renowned for being the world's bravest fighters, and their women famed for being the world's greatest beauties. At the heart of the Shura clan's beliefs lies the legend of the Three Thousand Worlds. The legends speaks of three thousand secret worlds, which upon entering, one must face various levels of psychic and physical trials and advance to world after world upon victory. The people of the Shura clan had discovered a way to enter these worlds, and only those who have conquered over three hundred worlds could be granted the title of Asura, raising them to the level of a demigod in the eyes of their people. However, in the innumerable eons of Shura history, none managed to conquer the fabled three thousand world, until Martis appeared.

With ambition and determination capable of shaking the very heavens, Martis trained every day of his life to become stronger, tirelessly advancing through the secret worlds, each personal breakthrough against the legions of demons granting him a sense of unique personal satisfaction. No one knew how many years passed before Martis reached the final and most hard core secret world. However, his desire for glory drove him onward. After numerous defeats, he fell into despair, but even then, he refused to give up. He transformed his sheer thirst for victory into his twin blades of righteous fury, now known as the Asura's Teeth, and for countless days and nights, battled against the demons of the final world with strength and determination that had never been seen before on the mortal plane. He finally became the first Asura to defeat the three thousand secret worlds and has ever since been regarded as the 'Asura King', ruling, more than tens of milions of aspiring Asura.

# Side Story

## Shura Clan and the Three Thousand Worlds

The Shura clan have thrived in isolation for countless generations, their men renowned for being the world's bravest fighters, and their women famed for being the world's greatest beauties. At the heart of the Shura clan's beliefs lies the legend of the Three Thousand Worlds. The legend speaks of three thousand secret worlds, which upon entering, one must face various levels of psychic trials and advance to world after world upon victory. The people of the Shura clan have discovered a way to enter these worlds, and only those who have conquered over three hundred worlds are granted the title of "Asura", raising them to the level of a demigod in the eyes of their people.

## Martis, First Asura King

However, in the innumerable eons of Shura history, none managed to conquer the fabled three thousand worlds, until Martis appeared. With ambition and determination capable of shaking the very heavens, Martis trained every day of his life to become stronger, tirelessly advancing through the secret worlds, each personal breakthrough against the legions of demons granting him a sense of unique personal satisfaction. No one knows how many years passed before Martis reached the final secret world, but his desire for glory drove him onwards. After numerous defeats, he fell into despair, but even then, he refused to give up. He transformed his sheer thirst for victory into twin blades of righteous fury, now known as the Asura's Teeth, and for countless days and nights, battled against the demons of the final world with strength and determination never seen before on the mortal plane. He finally became the first Asura to defeat the three thousand secret worlds and has since been regarded as 'King Asura', ruling tens of millions of aspiring Asura.

# Abilities

## Asura's Wrath

Each time Martis uses a skill, his Attack Speed will be increased by 30%, up to a maximum of 120%. This effect lasts 4 seconds. He gains (6 × Hero Level) extra Attack at full stacks.

## Asura Aura

Martis draws enemies to a fan-shaped area in front of him, dealing physical damage and slowing them by 40% for 2 seconds.

- Base Damage: 350 / 410 / 470 / 530 / 590 / 650
- Damage type: Physical Damage scaling with extra Physical Attack
- Additional effect: Movement Speed reduction on enemies

This skill makes Martis move forward a little, thus it counts as a blink skill despite not having a blink tag.

This skill is disabled while inside Minsitthar's ultimate.

This skill enables Phoveus' ultimate.

## Mortal Coil

Martis dashes in the target direction three times, each time dealing damage to enemies hit. Enemies hit by the first and third strike are also knocked back. The attack direction can be changed once using the Joystick.

Use Again: Martis dashes in the target direction, dealing damage to enemies in his path and knocking them back. Martis gains Control Immunity and 60% Damage Reduction when casting this skill.

- First phase Base Damage: 225 / 240 / 255 / 270 / 285 / 300
- Second phase Extra Damage: 300 / 320 / 340 / 360 / 380 / 400
- Effects: Blink, Control Immunity, Damage Reduction, knockback crowd control

Cooldown starts after casting the 1st or 2nd phase of the skill.

The 1st phase will trigger 3 stacks of Asura's Wrath, but only 1 stack on the Unique Passive of Brute Force Breastplate.

Interruption: This skill can only be interrupted during charging.

This skill is not immune to Suppression crowd control effects.

This skill can be disabled by Rooted effects and can be affected by knockback.

Upon casting, there will be a slight delay before the first slash occurs that does a quick knock-up effect to all opponents in front and beside Martis. This is then immediately followed up by another slash that only does damage to all opponents in front and beside Martis. After the second slash, there will be another slight delay before the last slash occurs that does a quick knock-up effect to all opponents near Martis.

The second phase of the skill immediately knocks all opponents away from Martis upon making contact on his body.

## Decimation

Martis lunges at a designated enemy hero, dealing damage (deals True Damage if the enemy's HP is below 50%). If the enemy is killed by this skill, Martis can cast the skill again within 10 seconds and gains 100% extra Movement Speed that gradually decays over 5 seconds. Each successive cast also increases the skill's damage by 15% (up to 30%).

- Base Damage: 800 / 900 / 1000
- Damage type: Physical Damage, becomes True Damage against enemies below 50% HP
- Effects: Movement Speed bonus, skill cooldown reset potential, executes low-HP enemies

When an enemy Hero's HP drops below 40%, a mark will appear beneath them.

There are two animations for his ultimate: he stabs the enemy with his blade after dashing in the first attempt; he jumps and stabs the enemy from above with his pair of blades in subsequent slays. Both of the animation does not differ in-game.

If Martis successfully eliminates a Hero equipped with Immortality, and the Unique Passive Immortal is still applied, he is able to recast the skill once more when the target Hero is resurrected by the Unique Passive Immortal.

The bonuses are also applied by enemy Heroes resurrected from Faramis' Cult Altar.

The cooldown only starts upon eliminating, or attempting to eliminate, an enemy Hero with this skill.

This skill can be interrupted during dashing.

This skill can be disabled by Rooted effects.

# Trivia

- The name Martis is derived from the Roman god of war, Mars. In Roman mythology, Mars was the god of war, battle, and violence, often associated with aggression and conflict. The name Martis retains this connection to warrior-like strength, power, and fighting prowess.
- Martis' Quote "3000 worlds and not a single worthy foe" was a bluff, he was actually defeated several times and was even put in despair.
- The term "3000 Worlds" is a hyperbolic reference to the 3000 Trials, where individuals must face and clear each trial (World or Level). Those who successfully complete 300 of these levels are considered to be at the level of a Demi-God in the eyes of the Asura Clan.
- After completing the 3000 Trials, Martis encountered the Demi-God Gatotkaca, and a fierce battle ensued. Both were equally matched in strength, and their fight alone created an entrance to the Timerift. After the 3000 Trials, Martis met a Demi-God Gatotkaca and fought, both were equal in strength and their fight alone opened an entrance to the timerift.
- Martis is canonically one of the most powerful hero in MLBB. Gatotkaca being his only equal and to stop the Ashura wrath.

metadata.hook: With ambition and determination capable of shaking the very heavens, Martis alone conquered the three thousand secret worlds to claim the title of Asura King.
metadata.teaser: From an isolated clan obsessed with the Three Thousand Worlds, Martis fights through endless trials and despair to become the first Asura King.
metadata.openingLine: The Shura clan have thrived in isolation for countless generations, in the Arcapada universe.
